positions of responsibility and trust
requiring a profound knowledge of
technology and a sound, practical
training. He was five years in the
United States civil service, a life
position, which he resigned to come
to Spokane, and during this period of
five years as irrigation engineer for
the United States government he con-
structed water supply for irrigation
systems as well as water works,
sewerage systems, pumping stations,
head works. syphon and pipe lines
For the past four years he has been
assistant engineer for the city of
Spokane.


Professor O. l.. Waller, graduate of
the University of Michigan in 1888,
Graduate of the University of Chi
cago, in 1856, Phi. ID. and PHI. NJ.
Hillsdale in 1887. He has been con-
nected for the last 10 or IL years
with the Pullman Agricultural college
in the state of Washington, also
short time superintendent of the Col
fax schools. For the last two or
three years he has spent a part of
his summer vacation visiting irriga-
tion enterprises to acquire some
practical knowledge along this line.
He has also been consulting engineer
for the Sunnyside Canal company. AS
professor of the Pullman Agricultural
college he has acquired data for the
United States government.


There seems to be no record what
ever of Professor Waller having ever
designed any hydraulic improvements
Of any kind or nature. lie has never
pumping station or built, or designed,
a reservoir in his life, nor can any
thing he found to show that he is n
graduate of any technical institution,
or has had any technical training
whatever. His record as a prominent
educator, a gentleman and a scholar
are flattering indeed and credit to
any man, but his record as hydrau-
lie engineer is not yet apparent.


Mr.. Otto Weile of this city is well
known and anyone familiar with the
controversy needs no information in
that line.


II. w. Scott of Seattle was born In
the Puget Sound country, began his
career as assistant to one of the well
known engineers and then became am
employe In the city engineers office
in Seattle. which position he has held
ever since. Thus IL will he seen that
Mr.. Scott's entire experience is linn
tied to the shores OF Puget souuI,
and no record can he found OF his
ever having constructed any hydrau-
lie improvements on his own rcspou-
sibility.
